response.justification: The candidate is an experienced senior web and full-stack engineer, but the profile fundamentally mismatches this role. The position hard-requires native-level Japanese, strong C for UNIX, and Linux kernel source-level work on embedded LAN-switch security features; the resume shows none of these. His C/C++ exposure is brief and dated (a six-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015), his listed languages are English, Romanian, and basic German, and there is no evidence of L2/L3 networking, switch products, or security technologies like MACsec or 802.1X.
strengths: Nearly 20 years of broad software experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded/low-level work
Low-latency systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Sociomantic and C/C++ IDE component work at National Instruments
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source authorship, and technical teaching/workshops
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency, which is a non-negotiable requirement for the role
No embedded networking, switch, or security-domain experience (MACsec, 802.1X, SNMP/NETCONF, kernel work)
C programming experience is minimal and roughly a decade old; recent career is entirely web/TypeScript-focused

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking/security or Linux kernel experience
Long, versatile senior engineering track record with some low-level exposure
